questions.prompt: Which symbol represents the vowel sound in the word "cat"?
questions.hint: This is a short, open front vowel — think of how wide you open your mouth.

questions.prompt: The word "see" uses the {{blank1}} vowel, while "sit" uses the {{blank2}} vowel.
questions.hint: One is long and tense, the other is short and lax.

questions.prompt: Match each IPA symbol to the word that contains that sound.
questions.hint: Say each word slowly and isolate the vowel sound in the middle.

questions.prompt: Which of these is a diphthong (a vowel that glides between two sounds)?
In British English, the word "bath" is typically pronounced with the {{blank1}} vowel.
questions.hint: This is the same long vowel as in "car" or "father".

questions.prompt: Match each vowel description to its correct IPA symbol.
questions.hint: "Close/open" refers to jaw height, "front/back" to tongue position.
